publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-07-03 18:34:20 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Consists mainly of reproductions of manuscripts and some printed material by or about mainly 16th- and 17th-century Italian scientists, amassed and indexed by Howard B. Adelmann.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: American embryologist and historian of science; professor of histology and embryology at Cornell University, 1921-1963.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: 112 v. of bound photocopies cataloged separately.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Adelmann research materials collection, #4703. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Consists mainly of reproductions of manuscripts and some printed material by or about mainly 16th- and 17th-century Italian scientists, amassed and indexed by Howard B. Adelmann in the course of his research on the history of anatomy, embryology and histology. Materials focus especially on 17th-century Italian anatomists Marcello Malpighi and Hieronymus Fabricius ab Aquapendente, but also include Giorgio Baglivi, Francesco Redi, Lorenzo Bellini, Donato Rossetti, Henry Oldenburg, Giovanni Domenico Cassini, Johannes Bohn, Vincenzo Busacchi, Giovanni Domenico Guglielmini, Albrecht von Haller, Luigi Ferdinando Marsili, Giambattista Morgagni, Antonio Francesco Ghiselli, Giovanni Alfonso Borelli, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes also original autograph letters by Marcello Malpighi (6 items) and Gaetano Atti (3 items); manuscript Tractatus in libros de generatione (17th century), on reproduction, sensation, and the theory of knowledge; typescript bibliography of the works of Fabricius; typescript with pencilled notes on manuscripts by Malpighi; notes and drafts for Adelmann's biography of Malpighi; and glass slides including portraits of Malpighi. Libraries where unbound photocopies were made are not identified; wood blocks and their origin are also unidentified.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Additional bound photocopies (cataloged individually) include works by and about Malpighi, and works by Ulisse Aldrovandi, Thomas Bartholin, Lorenzo Bellini, Georges Cuvier, Luigi Ferdinando Marsili, Giambattista Morgagni, Lorenz Oken, Francesco Redi, and others.

did.unittitle: Camper - De Analogia Inter Animalia et Stirpes
DeMaria - Elogio Storico di Luigi Rolando
Fracassati et al - Oratio in Funere Mariani Habita
Fulchignoni - Malpighi Sui Sensi del Gusto E. del Tatto
Gottsche - Uber den Bau der Retina
Gottsche - Ueber das Pigment des Auges
Hahn - Theorien der Epigenese U. Eveolution
Lilienfeld - Zur Morphologie U. Entwick, Der Geschlectsorgane
Lipari - Galenistarum See Malpighi- Conclusiones- Apologia
Malpighi - Ritrovamenta dei Manoscritti di Malpighi (Frati, 94-98)
Mauros - Uber Die Zeugung in all Gemeinen
Medici - Vita e Scritti Degli Anatomici Bologna
Neviani - Lettera di Marsili a Malpighi
Plagge - Ovi Mammalium See Cazin - Estomac des Oiseaux
Roesslein - De Differentiis Inter Foetum et Adultum
Sbaraglia - De Recent. Med. Stud.- Gott
Sbaraglia - Dissertationes Quibus Moderna Medicorum Studia Ventilantur
